summary:
Based solely upon typical presentations for both COPD due to long-term smoking history as well as potential underlying cardiovascular effects such as arrhythmias indicated by unspecified but likely abnormal findings noted during an electrocardiogram performed recently; it would suggest either acute coronary syndrome requiring further investigation through troponin levels etc., OR simply stable angina depending more contextually provided later regarding exact nature/abnormalities found within said ECG results not detailed herein yet implied strongly enough via process-of-elimination logic applied above based purely off presented information alone without additional specifics known at this stage post-round-one analysis.
To refine into two concise sentences focusing only clinically pertinent details while adhering strictly per instructions:

Breathlessness suggests possible COPD related to his smoking habit along with increased likelihood of cardiac involvement evidenced indirectly though unelaborated abnormalities hinted-at in mentioned ECG indicating need for differential diagnostic workup considering these primary possibilities among others less immediately apparent initially outlined briefly earlier now summarized succinctly hereafter.
